name: Sierra Mar
highlights: Floor-to-ceiling glass 1,200 feet above the Pacific
Four-course prix fixe dinner
Prix-fixe lunch at a more accessible price point
Open to non-guests for both meals

dietary.notes: Tasting menu — dietary accommodations with advance notice

hours: Lunch and dinner; check seasonal schedule
reservations: essential — book weeks or months ahead

name: Nepenthe
highlights: Iconic Big Sur terrace since 1949
The Ambrosia burger — a pilgrimage food
Unobstructed Pacific horizon from the deck
Built on land Henry Miller once owned
reservations: walk-in only — arrive early or late to avoid waits

name: Big Sur Bakery
highlights: Converted house surrounded by redwoods
Wood-fired pastries
Fresh eggs, excellent coffee
Pastry case sells out — arrive early
Local institution
hours: Breakfast and lunch; Friday–Tuesday only (closed Wed–Thu)

name: Big Sur River Inn Restaurant
highlights: Riverside deck over the Big Sur River
Patrons sit in chairs in the river during warm months
Gathering place for the local community
Classic American fare, cold drinks
hours: Lunch and dinner daily; check seasonal hours

name: Aubergine
highlights: One Michelin star
Tasting menu format
Intimate dining room
Wine program anchored on local producers
The special occasion restaurant of the orbit

dietary.notes: Tasting menu — dietary accommodations with advance notice

name: La Bicyclette
highlights: Seasonal menu, wood-fired oven
French bistro sensibility with California ingredients
Mushroom appetizer regularly on best-of lists
Walk-in bar seating available

name: Mission Ranch Restaurant
highlights: Carmel landmark owned by Clint Eastwood since 1986
Sheep meadow, Carmel River lagoon views
Point Lobos visible from dining room
Classic American — earned through authenticity

name: Carmel Belle
highlights: The morning anchor before heading south
Coffee, breakfast bowls, local provisions
Inside the Doud Craft Studios

name: Solstice at The Village Big Sur
highlights: Restaurant and cocktail lounge inside The Village Big Sur — a thoughtfully designed gathering place on Highway 1
Brunch and dinner service with a seasonal California coastal menu
Beautiful indoor-outdoor bar area — one of the best places to stop and linger on the coast
Share the compound with Big Sur Bodega and Word of Mouth Yoga — a full morning or evening here
hours: Brunch and dinner; check seasonal schedule

name: Deetjen's Big Sur Inn
highlights: Historic 1930s inn with an enchanting dining room — one of Big Sur's most atmospheric breakfast spots
No cell service, no WiFi — arrive here as a stranger, depart as a friend
Breakfast is the move: the kind of morning that makes you slow down before you even reach the trail
Built as a roadside stopover before Highway 1 was completed in 1937
hours: Breakfast and dinner; check seasonal schedule
reservations: Recommended for dinner; breakfast is more flexible

name: El Bistro by the Sea
highlights: Refined Mexican cuisine in the heart of Carmel — handmade tortillas, fresh salsas, creative brunch
Chilaquiles, birria, fish tacos, and a French toast that has no business being this good
Modern sophistication with deep Mexican culinary tradition — not a taqueria, not fusion, just excellent
Pet-friendly; outdoor seating available

dietary.notes: Many naturally gluten-free options; corn tortillas throughout

hours: Mon–Wed, Fri–Sat: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM. Closed Thu & Sun.
